title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: geosR package provides generalized geostatistical workflow calculate spatial resources (volume, metal/content tonnage, average grades). vignette demonstrates complete, end--end evaluation using generated synthetic data, reflecting best practices Senior Geoscientist.

what: 1. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) & Synthetic Data Generation
title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: real-world scenario, import drillhole data via st_read st_as_sf. , synthesize realistic spatial distribution representing nickel laterite deposit.
code: set.seed(42)  # 1. Generate a generic project boundary (Area of Interest) bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin=0, xmax=1000, ymin=0, ymax=1000), crs=32748) area <- st_as_sf(st_as_sfc(bbox)) area$ID <- "Block_A"  # 2. Generate random drillhole points across the area pts <- st_sample(area, size = 150, type = "random") drill_data <- st_as_sf(pts)  # 3. Simulate geological variables (Grade and Thickness) # We use a spatial trend (higher grades in the NE) + random noise coords <- st_coordinates(drill_data) trend <- (coords[,1] + coords[,2]) / 2000  drill_data$grade <- rnorm(150, mean = 1.5 + trend, sd = 0.3) drill_data$thickness <- rnorm(150, mean = 5 + (trend * 5), sd = 1.5)  # 4. Clean outliers (Best Practice: Remove spurious data before modeling) # We use geosR's built-in outlier removal on the raw vectors cln_grade <- no_outlier(drill_data$grade)  # Visualize the raw drillhole grade distribution ggplot(drill_data) +   geom_sf(aes(color = grade), size = 3) +   scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Synthetic Drillhole Data (Ni % Grade)")

title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: rigorous geostatistical estimate requires well-fitted variogram. model spatial continuity grade variable.
code: # Fit a spherical variogram model # Note: In practice, parameters like 'cutoff' and 'sill' are iteratively refined via EDA. var_model <- fit_var(   data = drill_data,    formula = grade ~ 1,    cutoff = 600,    width = 50,    model_type = "Sph",   sill = var(drill_data$grade) * 0.8, # Best practice: estimate sill from sample variance   range = 300 )  # Plot the experimental variogram and the fitted model plot(var_model$variogram, var_model$model, main="Spherical Variogram Model: Grade")

title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: variogram established, generate block model grid predict attributes un-sampled locations.
code: # Initialize a 25x25m block model grid calc_grid <- st_make_grid(area, cellsize = 25)  # Kriging for Grade kriged_grade <- est_krige(   data = drill_data,    formula = grade ~ 1,   grid = calc_grid,    vgm_model = var_model$model,   maxdist = 400, # Limit search radius   nmin = 3       # Require at least 3 drillholes for an estimate ) #> [using ordinary kriging]  # Kriging for Thickness (using same spatial model for simplicity) kriged_thick <- est_krige(   data = drill_data,    formula = thickness ~ 1,   grid = calc_grid,    vgm_model = var_model$model,   maxdist = 400,   nmin = 3 ) #> [using ordinary kriging]  # Visualize the kriged Grade predictions grade_raster <- as(st_rasterize(kriged_grade["var1.pred"]), "SpatRaster") plot(grade_raster, main = "Kriged Block Model (Grade %)") plot(st_geometry(area), add=TRUE, border="red", lwd=2)

title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: calculate total metric tonnage contained within boundary (area). apply specific gravity (density) 1.6 material type (typical laterite).
code: # Convert the sf predictions directly to terra SpatRasters for map algebra thick_raster <- as(st_rasterize(kriged_thick["var1.pred"]), "SpatRaster")  # Execute core resource calculation my_resources <- calc_res(   raster_grade = grade_raster,    raster_thickness = thick_raster,    area = area,    density = 1.6 )  # Review the tabular output (Polygon by Polygon) knitr::kable(my_resources$table, digits = 2, format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: senior geologist’s job doesn’t end estimation. must evaluate modeled resources actual mining recovery build confidence factors. Note: recovery factor < 1.0 indicates -estimation block model.
code: # Assume the plant reported 15,000 tonnes of metal content recovered from this block eval_table <- ev_rest(my_resources$table, actual_production = 15000)  # Display the reconciliation factor knitr::kable(eval_table[, c("ID", "metal_content", "actual_production", "recovery_factor")],               digits = 3, format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

title: geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation
text: Finally, generate standardized, presentation-ready maps management reporting.
code: # Generate map utilizing tmap architecture plot_res(   tonnage_raster = my_resources$raster,    area = area,    title = "Estimated Metal Content Distribution",   subtitle = "Block A" ) #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()` #>  #>  #> ── tmap v3 code detected ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> [v3->v4] `tm_raster()`: instead of `style = "kmeans"`, use col.scale = #> `tm_scale_intervals()`. #> ℹ Migrate the argument(s) 'style', 'palette' (rename to 'values') to #>   'tm_scale_intervals(<HERE>)' #> [v3->v4] `tm_raster()`: use `col_alpha` instead of `alpha`. #> [v3->v4] `tm_raster()`: migrate the argument(s) related to the legend of the #> visual variable `col` namely 'title' to 'col.legend = tm_legend(<HERE>)' #> [v3->v4] `tm_layout()`: use `tm_title()` instead of `tm_layout(main.title = )` #> [plot mode] fit legend/component: Some legend items or map compoments do not #> fit well, and are therefore rescaled. #> ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling.

what: The R Package for Geosciences Problems
title: The R Package for Geosciences Problems
text: geosR R package containing generalized functional wrappers resource estimation, exploratory data analysis, kriging. simplifies pipeline drillhole points evaluated resource tonnages wrapping gstat, sf, terra together best-practice standard names.

title: The R Package for Geosciences Problems
text: Spatial Modeling (fit_var, est_krige): Easily fit variograms raw geoscientific point data generate interpolated block models. Resource Evaluation (calc_res, ev_rest): Spatially intersect block models boundaries, calculate metric volume, configure density adjustments arbitrary commodities, evaluate reconciliation recovery factors. Mapping (plot_res): Leverage built-tmap configurations generate presentation-ready spatial maps. Statistical Helpers (backtr, nscore, no_outlier): Transform analyze data distributions directly.

title: The R Package for Geosciences Problems
text: can install development version geosR directly GitHub:
code: # Install devtools if you haven't already # install.packages("devtools")  devtools::install_github("ghoziankarami/geosR", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: The R Package for Geosciences Problems
text: core package documented interactive vignettes function manuals. Since package utilizes GitHub Actions automated documentation, can browse interactive tutorial online: 👉 geosR GitHub Pages site can also read vignette directly inside R session installation:

what: Back Transform Function from Gaussian distribution back to Normal — backtr
title: Back Transform Function from Gaussian distribution back to Normal — backtr
text: Originally written Ashton Shortridge, May/June, 2008

title: Back Transform Function from Gaussian distribution back to Normal — backtr
Back Transform Function from Gaussian distribution back to Normal — backtr
text: scores Numeric vector normal scores back-transformed. nscore normal score object (typically nscore() function). tails Character specifying extrapolation behavior extreme values. Options: 'none': extrapolation; defaults back initial min max data. 'equal': Assumes symmetric distribution mean; scales linearly. 'separate': Calculates independent standard deviations values /mean. draw Logical; TRUE, generates plot transform function.

title: Back Transform Function from Gaussian distribution back to Normal — backtr
text: numeric vector back-transformed original values.

title: Calculate Resources — calc_res
text: Calculates volume, grade, metal content (tonnage) resource block. calculation generalized commodity, utilizing geometric volume grade block models.

title: Calculate Resources — calc_res
text: raster_grade SpatRaster containing estimated grade values. raster_thickness SpatRaster containing estimated thickness values. area sf polygon outlining resource calculation boundary. density Numeric, bulk density specific gravity material (default = 1.0).

title: Calculate Resources — calc_res
text: list containing raster calculated metal content (tonnage) table summarizing area, expected volume, average grade, total metal content per polygon.

title: Estimate Ordinary Kriging — est_krige
text: Wraps around gstat::krige() generate kriging output specified sf grid bounds. handles spatial conversions automatically.

title: Estimate Ordinary Kriging — est_krige
code: est_krige(data, formula, grid, vgm_model, maxdist = 300, nmin = 3, omax = 1)

title: Estimate Ordinary Kriging — est_krige
text: data sf object containing drillhole data. formula formula specifying variable model (e.g., grade ~ 1). grid sf grid object predict. Can created using sf::st_make_grid(). vgm_model fitted variogram model (typically model output fit_var()). maxdist Numeric, maximum distance look data points. nmin Numeric, minimum number data points estimate. omax Numeric, maximum number data points per octant.

title: Estimate Ordinary Kriging — est_krige
text: sf object containing kriged predictions (var1.pred) variances (var1.var).

title: Evaluate Resource Reconciliation — ev_rest
text: Compares calculated resource model actual production (realization) values generate recovery factor "Koefisien Hasil".

title: Evaluate Resource Reconciliation — ev_rest
text: res_table data.frame tibble, typically table output calc_res(). actual_production Numeric, actual observed production values.

title: Evaluate Resource Reconciliation — ev_rest
text: original resource table appended columns actual_production recovery_factor.

title: Fit a Variogram Model — fit_var
text: function automates fitting variogram model dataset given parameters, suitable various commodities geological variables (e.g., grade, thickness).

title: Fit a Variogram Model — fit_var
code: fit_var(   data,   formula,   cutoff = 250,   width = 50,   model_type = "Sph",   sill = 0.02,   range = 150,   anisotropy = c(45, 0.5),   tol_hor = 22.5 )

title: Fit a Variogram Model — fit_var
text: data sf object containing drillhole sampling data. formula formula specifying variable model (e.g., grade ~ 1). cutoff Numeric, maximum distance variogram. width Numeric, lag width variogram. model_type Character, type variogram model (default "Sph" Spherical). sill Numeric, anisotropy sill initial sill. range Numeric, model range. anisotropy Numeric vector length 2 representing direction anisotropy ratio. tol_hor Numeric, horizontal tolerance angle (default: 22.5).

title: Fit a Variogram Model — fit_var
text: list containing experimental directional variogram (variogram), fitted variogram model (model), initial model parameters (initial_model).

what: Remove Outliers using IQR Method — no_outlier
title: Remove Outliers using IQR Method — no_outlier
text: Cleans numeric vector removing extreme positive negative outliers based Interquartile Range (IQR) method (Q1/Q3 +/- 1.5*IQR).

title: Remove Outliers using IQR Method — no_outlier
Remove Outliers using IQR Method — no_outlier
text: num Numeric vector cleaned.

title: Remove Outliers using IQR Method — no_outlier
text: numeric vector outliers removed.

title: Gaussian Transformation (Normal Score) — nscore
text: Originally written Ashton Shortridge, May/June, 2008.

title: Gaussian Transformation (Normal Score) — nscore
text: x Numeric vector transform.

title: Gaussian Transformation (Normal Score) — nscore
text: list containing: nscore normalized score vector. trn.table data frame mapping sorted original x values sorted nscore values.

title: Plot Resource Map — plot_res
text: Uses tmap generate standardized spatial mapping plots, tonnage distribution block.

title: Plot Resource Map — plot_res
code: plot_res(   tonnage_raster,   area,   title = "Resource Estimation",   subtitle = "",   col_palette = "Spectral" )

title: Plot Resource Map — plot_res
text: tonnage_raster SpatRaster representing calculated metal content resources per grid cell. area sf polygon outlining resource boundary. title Character, main title plot. subtitle Character, subtitle panel label content (e.g., total calculated resources). col_palette Character, color palette raster mapping. Default "Spectral".

title: Plot Resource Map — plot_res
text: tmap plot object suitable viewing export.
